name: code-quality
description: Expert at TypeScript strict mode, linting, formatting, code review standards. Use when checking code quality, fixing type errors, or enforcing standards.
capabilities: Enforces TypeScript strict mode compliance standards
Identifies type errors and suggests proper fixes
Detects anti-patterns like any types and ts-ignore usage
Reviews custom hooks for proper typing patterns
author: CrazySwami
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing TypeScript coding guidelines and examples. All 46 static findings are false positives triggered by markdown code fences, documentation examples, and metadata hashes. No executable code, network calls, or risky operations exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Code quality issues waste debugging time and cause production bugs. This skill provides comprehensive TypeScript guidelines including strict mode enforcement, type safety patterns, and anti-pattern detection.
limitations: Designed for TypeScript and React TypeScript projects only
Cannot fix runtime logic errors or business logic issues
Complex type inference issues may require manual resolution
Does not replace ESLint or automated testing

useCases.title: Maintain Consistent Code Standards
useCases.description: Ensure all team members follow the same TypeScript patterns and conventions across the entire codebase.

useCases.title: Speed Up Quality Reviews
useCases.description: Quickly identify TypeScript violations, missing types, and anti-patterns during code review.

useCases.title: Learn TypeScript Best Practices
useCases.description: Get guidance on proper TypeScript patterns, type safety, and avoid common beginner mistakes.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Type Check
promptTemplates.scenario: Find type errors in a file
promptTemplates.prompt: Check this TypeScript file for type errors and suggest fixes.

promptTemplates.title: Component Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this React component for TypeScript best practices, proper typing, and anti-patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Strict Mode Migration
promptTemplates.scenario: Enable strict mode in a project
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me enable TypeScript strict mode and fix all resulting type errors in this codebase.

promptTemplates.title: Custom Hook Typing
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a TypeScript custom hook with proper typing including options interface and return type for this functionality.

bestPractices: Enable strict mode in tsconfig.json for maximum type safety
Use explicit return types on exported functions for better API documentation
Replace any types with unknown and implement proper type guards
antiPatterns: Using ts-ignore to suppress errors instead of fixing the underlying type issue
Type assertions like 'data as any' to escape the type system
Leaving implicit any types in function parameters

faq.question: Which TypeScript projects does this work with?
faq.answer: Works with any TypeScript or React TypeScript project. Best results with strict mode enabled in tsconfig.json.

faq.question: Can it fix all TypeScript errors automatically?
faq.answer: It identifies and suggests fixes for common errors. Complex type inference issues may need manual review.

faq.question: How does it work with ESLint?
faq.answer: Complements ESLint by providing additional TypeScript-specific quality checks and pattern guidance.

faq.question: Is my code data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a documentation-only skill that provides guidelines without storing or transmitting your code.

faq.question: What if the suggested fix does not work?
faq.answer: Each suggestion includes alternative approaches. Ask for clarification if the first solution does not apply.

faq.question: How is this different from compiler errors?
faq.answer: Goes beyond compiler errors to enforce best practices, consistent patterns, and team coding standards.
